name: Amnioinfusion for meconium-stained amniotic fluid
summary: Across 23 randomized trials, prophylactic intrapartum amnioinfusion for meconium-stained amniotic fluid roughly halved the odds of meconium aspiration syndrome (MAS). The effect is heterogeneous: the largest, best-monitored trial (Fraser 2005) found no benefit, while smaller trials in limited-surveillance settings found large reductions.
description: A meta-analysis (Davis 2023, AJOG) of 24 RCTs (5994 participants) of prophylactic transcervical amnioinfusion in labor complicated by moderate-to-thick MSAF. The primary outcome was MAS. Each contributing trial's odds ratio is entered from the review's Figure 2 and pooled live; the review excluded Sadovsky 1989 from the MAS analysis, so 23 trials contribute here.
interpretation: The pooled odds ratio is driven down by many small trials and pulled up by Fraser 2005 (986 vs 989, OR 1.41), the only large multinational trial with continuous monitoring and standardized care—hence the substantial heterogeneity (I² ≈ 56%). Benefit was larger in settings with limited peripartum surveillance and in developing countries (subgroup ORs 0.20 and lower), and smaller where continuous monitoring was standard. The review also reported reductions in meconium below the cords, low Apgar, neonatal acidosis, cesarean delivery, and NICU admission, but not postpartum endometritis (see methodology). Absolute MAS rates in the review were 3.7% with amnioinfusion vs 9.5% without.
methodologyNotes: Primary outcome only (MAS) is encoded here, because this is the one outcome the review reports at the per-trial level (Figure 2); the secondary outcomes appear only as review-level pooled estimates (Table 3) with no per-study breakdown in the article, so they cannot be re-pooled from constituents and are described in prose rather than entered as a single pooled 'study'. Each trial is entered as its published odds ratio with 95% CI and pooled live by random-effects (DerSimonian–Laird) on the log-odds scale—nothing is hardcoded; our pooled OR reproduces the review's 0.33 (95% CI 0.21–0.51). Sadovsky 1989 is omitted because the review excluded it from the MAS forest. The review's per-trial CIs are log-symmetric Wald intervals (lower = point²/upper for every study), so the two trials whose lower bound prints as 0.00—Macri 1992 and Choudhary 2010—are entered with the implied lower bounds 0.0052 and 0.0057 rather than an unusable zero; Macri's value is corroborated by its raw counts (MAS 0/85 amnioinfusion vs 5/85 control). The review's Figure 2 labels three trials differently from its reference list: 'Gupta 2016' is Prachi et al 2016, 'Rathore 2002' is Rathor et al 2002, and the figure years for Choudhary (2009) and Elsersy (2016) differ from the published years (2010, 2017); citations and years here follow the published references. Odds ratios are pooled as published; because MAS is uncommon (~3–9%) the odds ratio approximates the risk ratio.

outcomes.label: Meconium aspiration syndrome
outcomes.description: Neonatal meconium aspiration syndrome as defined in each trial (definitions varied—clinical, CXR-based, or oxygen/ventilation criteria).

studies.author: Adam
studies.citation: Adam K, Cano L, Moise KJ. The effect of intrapartum amnioinfusion on the outcome of the fetus with heavy meconium stained amniotic fluid. Proceedings of 9th Annual Meeting of the Society of Perinatal Obstetricians; 1989; New Orleans, LA. 1989:438.
studies.doseRegimen: Single infusion of 1000 mL

studies.author: Bhosale
studies.citation: Bhosale R, Patil S, Patil Y, Kshirsagar N. Randomised clinical trial of amnioinfusion and caesarean section in cases of thick meconium stained amniotic fluid and neonatal outcome. Int J Recent Trends Sci Technol 2015;13:511-5.
studies.doseRegimen: Normal saline

studies.author: Choudhary
studies.citation: Choudhary D, Bano I, Ali SM. Does amnioinfusion reduce caesarean section rate in meconium-stained amniotic fluid? Arch Gynecol Obstet 2010;282:17-22.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 500 mL over 30 min, then 2 mL/min
studies.endpointDefinition: CXR and clinical course consistent with MAS.
studies.notes: Modified Jadad 6. Lower CI prints as 0.00; entered as the implied log-symmetric bound 0.0057 (=0.04²/0.28). Figure 2 labels this trial '2009'.

studies.author: Cialone
studies.citation: Cialone PR, Sherer DM, Ryan RM, Sinkin RA, Abramovici JS. Amnioinfusion during labor complicated by particulate meconium stained amniotic fluid decreases neonatal morbidity. Am J Obstet Gynecol 1994;170:842-9.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 600 mL over >1 h, then 150 mL/h
studies.endpointDefinition: MAS categories (subclinical, mild, or medium) based on O2 requirement and duration.

studies.author: Elsersy
studies.citation: Elsersy M. Utility of amnioinfusion in deliveries complicated by meconium stained liquor: a randomized controlled trial. Int J Reprod Contracept Obstet Gynecol 2017;6:65-71.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 500 mL over 30 min, then 300 mL at 3 mL/min
studies.endpointDefinition: RDS within a few hours of birth with compatible CXR finding.
studies.notes: Modified Jadad 5. Figure 2 labels this trial '2016'.

studies.author: Eriksen
studies.citation: Eriksen NL, Hostetter M, Parisi VM. Prophylactic amnioinfusion in pregnancies complicated by thick meconium. Am J Obstet Gynecol 1994;171:1026-30.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 800 mL over >1 h, then 180 mL/h

studies.author: Fonseca
studies.citation: Fonseca A, Dwivedi A. A randomised single blind control study to assess the effect of intrapartum amnioinfusion on maternal caesarean section rate and fetal outcome in a tertiary care centre. Int J Reprod Contracept Obstet Gynecol 2017;6:4347-50.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 500 mL over 30 min

studies.author: Fraser
studies.citation: Fraser WD, Hofmeyr J, Lede R, et al. Amnioinfusion for the prevention of the meconium aspiration syndrome. N Engl J Med 2005;353:909-17.
studies.doseRegimen: 800 mL saline at 20 mL/min over 40 min
studies.endpointDefinition: MAS adjudicated by a panel of 3 blinded neonatologists.
studies.notes: Modified Jadad 7. Largest trial; continuous monitoring and standardized care.

studies.author: Gavali
studies.citation: Gavali, Shinde S, Aher G, Mhaske S. Study of role of intrapartum transcervical amnioinfusion in meconium stained amniotic fluid. Indian J Basic Appl Med Res 2017;6:389-97.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 250 mL over 20-30 min, then 10-20 mL/min up to 600 mL

studies.author: Prachi
studies.citation: Prachi, Reena P, Priya BK, Swati T, Ruchi S, Sarita M. Study of perinatal outcome of amnio infusion during labor in meconium stained amniotic fluid. Scholars J Appl Sci Med 2016;4:2048-51.
studies.doseRegimen: 600 mL sterile saline over 30 min, then fluid until liquor cleared (max 1 L)
studies.notes: Modified Jadad 3. Labeled 'Gupta 2016' in the review's Figure 2.

studies.author: Hofmeyr
studies.citation: Hofmeyr GJ, Gülmezoglu AM, Buchmann E, et al. The collaborative randomised amnioinfusion for meconium project (CRAMP): 1. South Africa. Br J Obstet Gynaecol 1998;105:304-8.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 800 mL at 15 mL/min, then 3 mL/min maintenance
studies.endpointDefinition: Diagnosed clinically by a blinded pediatrician.

studies.author: Ilagan
studies.citation: Ilagan MKN, Kazzi GS, Liang K, Womack S, Bronsteen R. Transcervical amnioinfusion for prevention of meconium aspiration. Pediatr Res 1992;31.
studies.doseRegimen: 500 mL of NS

studies.author: Macri
studies.citation: Macri CJ, Schrimmer DB, Leung A, Greenspoon JS, Paul RH. Prophylactic amnioinfusion improves outcome of pregnancy complicated by thick meconium and oligohydramnios. Am J Obstet Gynecol 1992;167:117-21.
studies.doseRegimen: Warmed saline 500 mL, then 250-500 mL to keep AFI >10 cm
studies.population: United States; thick meconium and oligohydramnios
studies.notes: Modified Jadad 7. Raw MAS counts 0/85 (amnioinfusion) vs 5/85 (control); lower CI prints as 0.00, entered as the implied bound 0.0052 (=0.09²/1.57).

studies.author: Mahomed
studies.citation: Mahomed K, Mulambo T, Woelk G, Hofmeyr GJ, Gülmezoglu AM. The collaborative randomised amnioinfusion for meconium project (CRAMP): 2. Zimbabwe. Br J Obstet Gynaecol 1998;105:309-13.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 500 mL over 30 min, then 500 mL at 30 drops/min

studies.author: Moodley
studies.citation: Moodley J, Matchaba P, Payne AJ. Intrapartum amnioinfusion for meconium-stained liquor in developing countries. Trop Doct 1998;28:31-4.
studies.doseRegimen: 0.9% NS 10-15 mL/min

studies.author: Puertas
studies.citation: Puertas A, Paz Carrillo M, Moltó L, Alvarez M, Sedeño S, Miranda JA. Meconium-stained amniotic fluid in labor: a randomized trial of prophylactic amnioinfusion. Eur J Obstet Gynecol Reprod Biol 2001;99:33-7.
studies.doseRegimen: 0.9% NS 600 mL in first hour, then 180 mL/h

studies.author: Rathor
studies.citation: Rathor AM, Singh R, Ramji S, Tripathi R. Randomised trial of amnioinfusion during labour with meconium stained amniotic fluid. BJOG 2002;109:17-20.
studies.doseRegimen: NS 500 mL over 30 min, then 500 mL at 3 mL/min
studies.endpointDefinition: RDS within a few hours of birth with compatible CXR finding.
studies.notes: Modified Jadad 5. Labeled 'Rathore 2002' in the review's Figure 2.

studies.author: Sood
studies.citation: Sood M, Charulata, Dimple, Aggarwal N, Faridi MM. Amnioinfusion in thick meconium. Indian J Pediatr 2004;71:677-81.
studies.doseRegimen: 1 L saline over 30-45 min

studies.author: Spong
studies.citation: Spong CY, Ogundipe OA, Ross MG. Prophylactic amnioinfusion for meconium-stained amniotic fluid. Am J Obstet Gynecol 1994;171:931-5.
studies.doseRegimen: 600 mL bolus, then 3 mL/min
studies.endpointDefinition: Meconium below the cords with RDS requiring oxygen or ventilation and no evidence of sepsis.

studies.author: Thakkar
studies.citation: Thakkar P, Aiyer S. Effect of amnioinfusion on outcome of neonates born with meconium stained amniotic fluid. Natl J Integr Res Med 2012;3.
studies.doseRegimen: NS/LR at 100 mL/min until liquor cleared (up to 1000 mL)

studies.author: Vachhani
studies.citation: Vachhani N, Shah J, Goel G, Mehta M, Desai A, Dalal M. Intrapartum amnioinfusion in meconium stained amniotic fluid—a randomized control study in a tertiary care hospital. Natl J Community Med 2015;6:442-4.
studies.doseRegimen: 500 mL NS, then 180-200 mL/h

studies.author: Wenstrom
studies.citation: Wenstrom KD, Parsons MT. The prevention of meconium aspiration in labor using amnioinfusion. Obstet Gynecol 1989;73:647-51.
studies.doseRegimen: 1 L NS over 20-40 min, repeated every 6 h

studies.author: Yellayi
studies.citation: Yellayi ASS, Aruna S, Devi DH. Role of amnioinfusion in prevention of meconium aspiration syndrome. Natl J Clin Sci 2017;5:272-7.
studies.doseRegimen: 600 mL NS over 1 h, then 180 mL/h
